text:
Location scouting should never be left out! This is an example of how @rubenscott prepares for a shoot 🎥 DP: @ariel_plischuk 

Ruben shares:
✨ *“One thing I never skip: location scouting.
It’s more than just checking out a spot—it’s where the whole vision starts to click. Scouting helps me figure out framing, lens choices, and how to bring the story to life before the camera even rolls.

For me, prep is half the work and all the confidence. Don’t skip it, and definitely don’t skip the scout.”*

Pro tip: apps like Artemis Pro are great for testing lens lengths, framing, and building your shot plan before the shoot day. 👌

text:
Behind the scene 🎬 

Before these visuals appeared on the large public screens in Shibuya, we rebuilt parts of the city in 3D to study the real architectural context, screen positions, and how each piece would actually be seen from the street.

For us, this process was never only about creating content that plays on a screen.
What mattered more was understanding how the work would exist inside the real city — how pedestrians would approach it, how perspective would shift from different viewpoints, how the surrounding buildings would affect depth and scale, and how motion could feel more convincing within the urban environment.

That is why we spent a significant part of the production process simulating real street viewpoints and redesigning each scene around those conditions.
We adjusted perspective, depth, scale, and movement based on how the visuals would be experienced in reality, not just how they looked on a monitor.
The goal was to make each piece feel more naturally connected to the architecture and to create visuals that could extend beyond the screen into the city itself.

This is part of the thinking and simulation behind the final work.

今回の映像を渋谷の街頭大型スクリーンに映し出す前に、私たちは街の一部を3D上で再構築し、建築の形状やスクリーンの位置関係、そして実際に通行人がどのような視点から作品を見るのかを細かく検証しました。

私たちにとって大切なのは、単にスクリーン用の映像を作ることではありません。
むしろ重要なのは、その映像が実際の都市空間の中でどう存在し、どう見えるのかということです。
人がどの方向から近づくのか、見る位置によってパースがどう変わるのか、周囲の建築によって奥行きやスケール感がどう感じられるのか。そうした要素が、最終的な映像の立体感やリアリティに大きく関わってきます。

そのため制作の初期段階から、私たちは実際の街頭視点をシミュレーションしながら、各シーンのパース、奥行き、スケール、動き方をあらためて設計していきました。
モニター上で綺麗に見えることだけではなく、現地で体験したときに、映像が建築や街の流れと自然につながって見えることを目指しています。

今回の投稿では、そうした最終作品の裏側にあるシミュレーションと制作過程の一部を紹介しています。

#chamberlabart #neoshibuyatv #tokyo #behindthescene #makingof

One day. One setup. Total precision. 
Director @boulawayne takes us behind the scenes of his latest project, developed in collaboration with @cinemotionrobotics

Director’s Notes 👇
Total precision using @cinemotionrobotics Ultra system, we combined robot track with a synchronized turntable, to create long motion controlled moves around the car.

A real technical feat, allowing for flawless multipass shots and an extreme level of control over every details.

Thx again to the MoCo Wizard @_ynnb and Dop @julienlascar and Post production @illogicstudios for their talents, creativity and dedication.

text:
a much ‘tidier’ behind the scene set that is able to show up here ☁️ while others we could barely reveal it as those look more like ‘disaster’ scenes 😂

While behind the scenes aren’t just shooting set scene like this but there are lots of prep work before u think we can actually just clicking the shutter button to get a perfect shot 😬

Below explains some about where do photography gets its costs 👀 

From brainstorming ideas, sourcing and choosing the right props, setting up the shooting set by adjusting the lighting, styling the product before we finally get to click on the camera buttons 😆

Not forgetting the post production of it like photo editing and the scene cleanings part lol 😂 It’s so much fun to us but the truth is A LOT OF WORKS!!! We usually ended up with painful bodies after photo-shooting day 🥲

But of course we always try our best to produce artwork that could bring values to our clients’ business and helping them to grow! 💙That’s what we will be proud of and worth all our sweats 💦

Episode 2 Behind the Scene of X-Treme Borneo Challenge 2.0 

Explore the unseen moments from Episode 2 of the Isuzu X-Treme Borneo Challenge 2.0.

Our brave challengers embarked on a remarkable journey from the urban elegance of Kota Kinabalu to the demanding landscapes of the Borneo Safari. These behind-the-scenes photos capture their first foray into the wilderness, showcasing the unmatched capabilities of the Isuzu D-Max. 

Witness the beginning of this extraordinary escapade and the might of the D-Max in action. Be inspired by their determination and share in their adventure.

📸 Plus, don’t forget to participate in our Watch & Win contest to grab your piece of the action with exclusive gifts by following the steps below:

Step 3️⃣ Stay tuned to Isuzu D-Max Facebook to find out if you’re a winner.

Find out more at www.IsuzuXtremeBorneoChallenge.com or click on the Bio Link.

text:
Here is how we make the famous Hungarian beef goulash. Swipe for video. 
  
Recipe: 
•  1 tbs vegetable oil
• 2 heads of brown onion
• 2 cloves of garlic
• 1 tsp of Piros Arany ( if you don't have no problem you will add paprika later anyway,and we did add a homemade paprika paste as well but you can leave it out) 
• 400g diced beef 
• 1 tsp bicarbonate of soda (it helps to soften the meat but is optinal can be left out) 
• 2.5 tbsp ground paprika
• 500ml water
• 2 Bay leafs 
• Salt and pepper 
• 1 beef stock pot 
• 1 vegetable stock pot
• 100ml red wine such as cabernet sauvignon. 

Method: 
Heat a little oil in a bigger pan than soften the onion and the garlic (if you using Red pepper paste add it now if you not you can skip this step) when the onions are softened add the beef and the bicarbonate of soda (please note that the bicarbonate of soda can foam) and cook until the beef is browned approx 5 minutes than take the pan off from the heat and stir in the ground paprika, it is important to remove the pan from heat because if the paprika burns it will have a bitter unpleasant taste, cook for further 2-3 minutes than add the water, stock pot's and  the seasoning and cook on medium heat for 3-4 hours covered, keep adding water to it as it evaporates always try to keep at least as much water on it that covers the meat, you can set it's consistency at the end of cooking if you like it thick don't add as much water before the end. Approx  15 minutes before the end of the cooking time add the red wine as well. Keep in mind the texture of the goulash should be not to watery but not to thick but it is up to taste really. 

Enjoy. 
.
.
Please note that this recipe is how we make this very versatile stew and it is how we learned from our family.

text:
My curiosity for nature was instilled in me by my father, an avid nature lover. We had a little garden where herbs, vegetables and fruits would be grown in rotation, but there were always a few plants that were permanent fixtures in our green sanctuary. The hibiscus plant was one of them.

The bold petals of the hibiscus flower always stood out against a sea of green, their glorious blooms punctuating the garden in various colours and shades, not unlike an inverted lantern. It is with this flower in mind that Thousand Petals was created.

The key ingredient in our velvety and moussy mask is the roselle flower, a member of the hibiscus family and the more subtle version of the hibiscus flower as we know it. It is more compact in form, but brims with nutrients and medicinal value. Traditionally steeped in hot water to make a tart and refreshing tea, the roselile flower has also made great strides in the world of green chemistry and demonstrated topical skin benefits – specifically its ability to exfoliate ever so gently yet efficiently.

The roselle flower might look pretty, but this beautiful ingredient packs a punch and Thousand Petals is a tribute to this flower I grew up knowing and loving. 

#behindthescenes #howwemakeit #exfoliatingmask 

📷: @tifftiff_studio

text:
We sit down with our lead designer to talk about what the design process looks like for an Evolve board. From initial sketches and prototyping parts, to sending a final board into production, there is many challenges and successes in creating a ground-up design. Full video is on the new eSkate Chat channel (link in story).
•
“Electric forms of transport are an interesting area of development. Aimed at high-end users, the Evolve Carbon Hadean is a premium version of an existing model that showcases a smart use of materials and design construct to reduce weight and bulk. The well-executed upgrade cleverly uses smart technology in the processing (including composite forged carbon fibre) and the electronics (including lithium-Ion battery cells electric motors) to ooze speed and performance. A standout project that ticks all the boxes for good design in this category. Well done.” ~ @gooddesignaus
•

text:
Let’s talk about our pork today!! Like our chicken, it takes us THREE days to make. 😍 Here’s what we do:
-Brine it for 48 hours in a mix of herbs and spices
-Season it with our secret rub
-Stack a mix of pork shoulder and belly on the spit (about a 70/30 mix), and then sear it for 3 hours. Because of how we layer it, you’ll get both shoulder and belly in every dish!
-Then, we steam it in a combi oven for 8 more hours. This makes it super juicy.
That’s it! Our pork - from ZERO to GYRO in just about 3 days. We hope you love it! 💙

text:
💛 Our Moonflower linen fabric was inspired by a combination of; Susie’s garden, where she has a true passion for botanical blooms, and from her everlasting love of Indian motifs.
…
…

A moonflower also known as ‘Belle de Nuit’ translating to Beauty of the Night 🌙 is a sweetly scented white flower which opens in the evening and will close it’s petals by the first morning light.

At dawn the delicately dense packed buds start to shape into a star, and by dusk they have rolled themselves up into a tight safe twist. The form and outline of this trellis flower enthused Susie to create a mottled sketch, which transpired into a simple yet stunning block pattern. Once the pattern was rotated, it created a timeless design; useful for interior decoration, such as #curtains and #blinds.

The scale of the moonflower has allowed us to build on this collection launching a selection of cushions and lampshades which we use alot.. They really are so easy to use everywhere!

text:
From this.... ➡️ To this! ⁠
⁠
Ever wondered how we get all that deep, complex, spicy flavour in our fiery Ginger Beer? 🔥 Well, buckle in for a Fizzy Pop crashcourse, Fizz Fam!⁠
⁠
It all starts with the finest quality root ginger, which we make sure to always buy whole. We press the root on site right here in East London, and steep it to release the natural spicy flavours within. This gives our Ginger Beer a bright, clean taste and natural spice without the need to add chilli or any artificial ingredients! ⁠
⁠
From there, we add freshly squeezed lemon juice, and blend with four different types of dark and light sugars to give a complex and unique flavour! The result? A warming and hearty flavour that's bound to stimulate the palate! ⁠
⁠
𝗗𝗜𝗗 𝗬𝗢𝗨 𝗞𝗡𝗢𝗪?: Our Ginger Beer was THE very first recipe Ed & Robyn made in their kitchen, to sell at local farmers' markets. We've come a long way since then, but our OG (that's Original Ginger 😉) remains refreshingly similar! ⁠
⁠

text:
How is Joie jewellery made?
All of our jewelry is small batch hand made, and we use an age-old technique call lost wax casting.
There are two main processes we use to create our waxes for casting:
1. We take a silicone mould from nature, then do a slip wax pouring of the mould. This is how we create our cedar leaves and sea shell designs, simple and beautiful just the way nature intended the design.

2. The other method is a little newer: after sketching a design, we create first a 2D CAD rendering of the design, then turn the 2D design into a 3D designs and then 3D print the wax. 
I love the CAD process, as I can easily make many changes to a designs, and sometimes create 30 iterations to get it just right. 
I also love exploring seashores and forest looking for that perfect object to cast.
.
.
.
.

BIG NEWS🚨Creatine isn’t just for muscle.
A newly published review analyzed decades of human, animal, and neuroimaging research to examine how creatine interacts with the brain — not hype, not stimulation, but bioenergetics.
The takeaway?

Creatine supports brain energy — especially when demand is elevated.

✔️ Sleep deprivation ✔️ Aging ✔️ Depression ✔️ Neurological stress

In metabolically stable, well-rested young adults? Effects are smaller. Why? Because creatine isn’t a stimulant. It’s energy support.
The research makes one thing clear: Brain impact depends on context, dosing strategy, and duration — even more than muscle does.

Creatine isn’t a shortcut. It’s a biologically essential compound for cellular energy regulation.
The question is no longer if creatine matters for brain health — It’s how to apply it intelligently.

Reference: Creatine Supplementation and the Brain: Have We Put the Cart Before the Horse? Journal of Dietary Supplements DOI: 10.1080/19390211.2026.2616440

text:
In @MrBeast’s newest video, contestants compete to be the last person standing inside a fully stocked grocery store with $250,000 on the line. Behind the scenes, our team helped supply the entire meat aisle.

That meant every steak, every cut of chicken, every pork chop, and every piece of seafood in the store came from Good Ranchers.

For us, the moment represents something bigger than product placement. For years, the meat industry has operated in a system where it’s surprisingly difficult for consumers to know where their food actually comes from. Products on grocery store shelves often travel thousands of miles before reaching the case, and origin labeling can be confusing at best.

Good Ranchers was built to change that.

We partner directly with American farms and ranches to deliver beef, chicken, pork, and wild-caught seafood that’s born, raised, and harvested in the United States. Our goal has always been simple: make it possible for families to know exactly where their meat comes from.

MrBeast’s videos regularly reach tens of millions of viewers within days of release, which means a lot of people are about to see something most Americans rarely encounter.

A meat aisle where you actually know what you’re buying.

What you see in that store should not be unusual. It should be the standard.

Until it is, we’ll keep building it ourselves.

text:
For the past few months, our team has been deeply embedded with the @SeminoleBooster, the fundraising, innovation, and development arm of the Florida State @Seminoles.

Last week we started pulling back the curtain on what we've been building behind the scenes. From everything I have seen working with them, I've been blown away with they operate. The way this group thinks about the future of college sports is unlike anything I've seen across the landscape. They're not worried about "keeping up,"  they're building a true, scalable business.

At @NOCAPSports, one of our biggest strengths has been our ability to move fast, pilot intelligently, and adapt to where the industry is heading without overextending. That approach is exactly what's driving results at Florida State. Our work with Seminole Boosters is already generating hundreds of thousands in annual recurring revenue, with multiple millions projected over the next 12 months, and we are only scratching the surface of the strategy we are building with them long term.

But the mission behind the numbers matters just as much. Our focus has been on building sustainable revenue streams for athletic departments - dollars that don't disappear when the donation cycle slows or the NIL market shifts. When a program has a stable, recurring revenue foundation, it creates real capacity to invest in athletes, not just their performance, but their long-term earning potential.

That's what we're building at FSU. What makes this partnership special isn't just the numbers. The Seminole Boosters have given our team full access to embed across everything they're doing, unlocking NOCAP's full capability suite across FSU athletes, fans, sponsors, and donors.

That kind of trust is rare, and we don't take it lightly. Endless appreciation to the entire Seminole team. Let's keep building 🧱 🚀

text:
For years this lived as one of the most talked about ideas in urban water…

Now there is a confirmed site at New York’s Pier 35 funding in place and treatment systems being engineered to operate in the East River.

We have been working behind the scenes along the way. Our team delivered the pilot water treatment system that helped validate the concept and we have now been selected to provide the full scale treatment system as @pluspoolny advances on Manhattan’s Lower East Side!

Why this project stands apart:

💧It draws water directly from the East River filters it through the pool walls and returns it cleaner in real time.

💧A 2024 pilot demonstrated capacity to purify up to one million gallons per day.

💧With 16 million dollars in New York State funding it has shifted from ambitious idea to committed public infrastructure.

Film has played a quiet but powerful role in getting it here. Early @Kickstarter videos carried the idea around the world and rallied thousands of backers. The Swim in the River campaign with @Heineken_US produced by @Tribeca  helped engage a global audience and ultimately helped catalyze a City RFP for an East River swim facility.

At @WEForg ’s WEFTEC this year the World Water Film Festival featured a documentary on the team behind + POOL and hosted a panel on how film can drive connection and action in water. A reminder that infrastructure moves with policy and engineering but also with story.

The first phase will open as a rectangular in river section with the remaining arms of the plus shaped design planned to follow.

I look forward to being on site in New York this summer with my colleagues and the + POOL team as this takes shape in the water. If you’re going to be around, reach out.

-Barron
@watermaxxing_ 

https://t.co/7CJFG6CXTt

text:
It’s Sunday and we hope everyone had a great weekend.

Our team has been heads down working on a lot behind the scenes. It’s difficult to share everything right now, but I want to give the community a few things to be excited about.

We recently started working with an incredible gaming development team. I can’t share specific details yet, but I can say it’s going to be fun, addictive, and full of the kind of adult humor our core holders love.

We also teased a mini game a while back with Cheese fighting pigeons. That is still actively being developed. We have a very unique plan for how it will be released, but I can’t share the details just yet.

On the social side, we had to rebrand our channels to Zero to Hero after TikTok shadow banned us. Apparently they think the logo is a bunny instead of a donkey. Regardless, the rebrand was something that needed to happen anyway. If we ever want to land a deal for an adult animated series, we wouldn’t be able to do that with Web3 or Playboy in the name. The good news is that our Web2 growth has been parabolic and Zero to Hero fits the broader vision very well.

We’re also working on an expansion collection that introduces new characters. One of our animated videos recently went viral, and we realized we need more characters for the adult animated series we’re building. I can’t share more about this yet, but we’re approaching it very strategically and people are going to love what we have planned.

The first limited edition Cheese plushy is currently being produced. I’m hoping to receive the first sample within the next few weeks. Once we confirm the quality meets our standards, we’ll move straight into production. It will be a limited supply at a fair price and available to both our Web2 audience and Web3 holders. There will also be a pool of claimable Cheese traits included, with a few 1/1 Cheese companions.

We’re also sourcing T shirt manufacturers right now and waiting on samples. Once we confirm the quality is where it needs to be, we’ll release our first design in two different colors. And yes, merch will also include claimable traits.

There’s a lot more in the pipeline, but I can’t share additional specifics right now. I’ve said this many times before: when we get quiet, that’s when you should be the most bullish. It means the team is heads down working on things that will increase the success, value, and growth of the brand.

We’ll probably need about another week and a half before I can host a space and take questions. Our team still needs time to meet internally and finalize several things.

text:
How We Make AI Animation Ads

Here's the exact 7-step process we use:

Step 1: Choose your style
Claymation, Pixar, yarn, realistic. Pick one before you touch a prompt. Everything downstream is built around this decision.

Step 2: Research in NotebookLM
Paste the product page URL. Let it learn everything. Features, benefits, the problem it solves. This is your research layer before a single prompt is written.

Step 3: Generate your prompt sequence
Use a prompt inside NotebookLM to build the ad framework. Beginning, middle, end, 60 seconds. Then move it to Claude or ChatGPT and go deeper. Individual image prompts for every shot in the sequence.

Step 4: Generate images in NanoBananaPro
Aim for 10 to 20 images depending on length. Refine with Claude if anything's off. Use Pinterest images as style references to mimic a specific look.

Step 5: Animate in Kling 3.0
Take each image pair. Prompt it to generate a seamless claymation transition between them. 1080p, 7 to 8 second clips. Speed up in post if needed.

Step 6: Voiceover and music
Write the script in the same Claude or ChatGPT session. It already knows the product. Voice in ElevenLabs. Background music in Suno.

Step 7: Stitch in CapCut
Mixed transitions between clips. Layer the voiceover and music. Export at 1080p.

That's a full AI ad. Start to finish.

Most brands are paying $3 to $5k for production to get the same result.

For the full step-by-step process with all prompts like, rt, and comment "ads".

text:
In summary, this is how we make decisions.  Your opponent, assailant, or whatever you choose to call the threat uses the same process.  To win a fight, understand the process, and learn to interupt his/her/its OODA LOOP.

The OODA loop (observe, orient, decide, act loop) is a decision-making model developed by United States Air Force Colonel John Boyd in the early 1970s. He applied the concept to the combat operations process, often at the operational level during military campaigns. It is often applied to understand commercial operations and learning processes.[1] The approach explains how agility can overcome raw power in dealing with human opponents.

The Four Stages of the OODA Loop:

Observe: Gather raw data and information from your environment using all your senses and tools, understanding the current situation.

Orient: This is the most crucial stage; you filter observations through your existing knowledge, experiences, cultural heritage, and mental models to form a meaningful understanding and context.

Decide: Based on your orientation, you choose the best course of action from your available options.

Act: You execute the decision, and this action then becomes new input for the next "Observe" phase, creating a continuous cycle.

text:
When you run EXPLAIN, PostgreSQL shows estimates, not execution.

Here’s what the main terms mean 👇

Cost
→ Planner’s estimated work
→ Not execution time

Rows
→ How many rows PostgreSQL expects
→ Based on statistics

Width
→ Average size of each row (bytes)
→ Affected by SELECT * vs needed columns

🎯 Interview Insight:
PostgreSQL chooses a plan based on estimated cost, not actual speed.

📌 If estimates are wrong → planner decisions can be wrong.

text:
Many beginners think SQL performance is only about indexes.
But PostgreSQL decides how to run your query first 👇

PostgreSQL uses a query planner.

When you run:
EXPLAIN

It shows:

How PostgreSQL will scan the table

Whether it uses an INDEX or full table scan

Estimated cost and rows

Without EXPLAIN:
You only see results.

With EXPLAIN:
You see how PostgreSQL thinks 🧠

🔍 EXPLAIN ANALYZE:

Executes the query

Shows actual time & rows

Helps compare estimated vs real performance

💡 Interview Tip:
Always say “I use EXPLAIN ANALYZE to understand query performance”.

📌 One-line takeaway:
EXPLAIN shows the plan. EXPLAIN ANALYZE shows the truth.

text:
BACK HANDSPRING -

The Back Handspring is one of those moves that when someone performs it you know that they’re not just good at Back Handsprings, they’re pretty athletic all around.  What makes a person athletic and capable of skills like the Back Handspring? At GMB we believe that the combination of strength, flexibility, and control builds the base to become athletic and more resilient. -

The following progressions demonstrated by GMB Trainer Eduardo @eorihuela will help you with your Back  Handspring training: -

1. Monkey 360 
2. Backwards Monkey 360 
3. Macaco 
4. Standing Macaco 
5. Back Handspring -

Having an experienced coach in a safe place to practice is also very important. You won’t want to just jump into it, you’ll want to develop the attributes and easier skills before attempting these more advanced movements. Things like Bear, Monkey, Frogger are great starters to build up to forward rolls, backwards rolls, Cartwheels, and Handstands.  We wrote an article on the pyramid of preparedness that we think you’ll really like. Check it out: https://gmb.io/athleticism/ -

text:
Did you know? 🔪 Slicing #chicken against the grain can help make the meat more tender 😍💪 But how? 

🍗 This technique is useful especially for tougher cuts like thighs and wings—by breaking the muscle fibers, the meat becomes softer and easier to chew. For thicker cuts, slicing 𝗮𝗴𝗮𝗶𝗻𝘀𝘁 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗴𝗿𝗮𝗶𝗻 ensures even cooking, preventing some parts from staying raw while others dry out. Plus, it increases the surface area exposed to seasoning or marinade, allowing flavors to penetrate more deeply.

When should you do it? 🤔 
It works well for thighs, wings, and chicken breasts, especially when cooked whole or in large pieces. If you plan to marinate the chicken, cutting against the grain will help it absorb flavors better. It’s important for recipes that require tender meat—if the cooking method might make the meat tough, slicing against the grain is a great way to prevent that.

Did you know it? 👇

text:
How to Prepare Matcha at home 🍵 ₊˚.༄

Here is a beginners guide if you’re new to making matcha. 
1) Bloom your Bamboo Whisk by soaking it in hot water for 2 minutes. This softens the prongs making it easier to whisk later on.
2) Measure 1tsp of ceremonial matcha powder. 
3) Sift! Matcha is prone to clumping over time. 
4) Add in 60ml of hot water (avoid boiling water as this will burn and make your matcha bitter).
5) Whisk away! Use gentle pressure and avoid pressing the bristles hard against the bowl as it may damage your chasen. 
6) For Matcha Latte: pour over 100ml of milk (3.4oz).

And enjoy! 

Let me know if this helped 🫶
Using @kurumatcha ceremonial Uji matcha.

text:
How to: Install a Tap

Installing a new tap on your Village Pottery Company ceramic water purifier is quick and straightforward. You will need a shifter or ratchet tool.

Steps

Insert the tap through the hole from the outside of the purifier, ensuring the stainless-steel disc and one white washer are in place.

From the inside, slide on the white washer, the second stainless-steel disc, and the nut.

Screw on the back nut and use a shifter or ratchet to tighten securely.

Check the tap is straight.

Tip
Please see the video in the final IG and FB slide. I find it easiest to hold the nut in place and twist the front of the tap until tight, then repeat while holding the nut with the shifter. This reduces awkward handling and gives a cleaner, firmer fit.

All Village Pottery Company taps and parts are covered by a 1-year warranty. Our taps are made to last (one of ours is still going strong after 10+ years), though wear and tear can vary over time.

If you have any questions or need replacement parts, we’re always happy to help.

text:
🥑🔥 ONE AVOCADO DRESSING. 6 EASY DISHES. SAVE THIS.

Creamy, fresh, and packed with flavor—this avocado dressing is perfect for bowls, wraps, and salads.

🧄 Avocado Dressing

Ingredients
1 ripe avocado
2 tbsp lime juice
2 tbsp olive oil
1/4 cup yogurt
1 clove garlic
1/2 tsp salt

Instructions
 1. Add avocado, yogurt, lime juice, olive oil, and garlic to a blender.
 2. Blend until smooth and creamy.
 3. Add salt and adjust consistency with a little water if needed.
 4. Taste and adjust seasoning.
 5. Chill before serving for best flavor.

🍽️ 6 WAYS TO USE IT

🥗 Green Salad

Ingredients
Mixed greens
Cucumber
Edamame
Pumpkin seeds

Instructions
 1. Combine all ingredients in a bowl.
 2. Drizzle avocado dressing.
 3. Toss gently and serve.

🌮 Taco Salad

Ingredients
Ground beef or beans
Lettuce
Corn
Black beans
Cheese
Tortilla chips

Instructions
 1. Cook beef or prepare beans.
 2. Assemble all ingredients in a bowl.
 3. Drizzle avocado dressing on top.
 4. Serve immediately.

🍗 Chicken Bowl

Ingredients
Grilled chicken
Quinoa or rice
Roasted peppers
Avocado slices

Instructions
 1. Arrange grains and vegetables in a bowl.
 2. Add sliced grilled chicken.
 3. Drizzle avocado dressing.
 4. Serve warm or chilled.

🌯 Veggie Wrap

Ingredients
Tortilla
Shredded carrots
Cucumber
Spinach
Bell peppers

Instructions
 1. Layer vegetables on tortilla.
 2. Drizzle avocado dressing.
 3. Roll tightly and slice.
 4. Serve fresh.

🥣 Grain Bowl

Ingredients
Brown rice or quinoa
Chickpeas
Roasted vegetables
Broccoli

Instructions
 1. Combine grains and toppings in a bowl.
 2. Add avocado dressing.
 3. Toss lightly or drizzle on top.
 4. Serve warm.

🥕 Dip for Vegetables

Ingredients
Carrots
Celery
Cucumber
Bell peppers

Instructions
 1. Arrange vegetables on a platter.
 2. Serve avocado dressing as a dip.
 3. Enjoy fresh and crisp.

text:
🍋🐟 ONE LEMON-DILL SAUCE. 6 SALMON DISHES. SAVE THIS.

Creamy, bright, and packed with fresh herbs — this velvety lemon-dill sauce is the perfect match for salmon in every form. From elegant Wellington to simple wraps, this one sauce does it all.

🧄 Lemon-Dill Salmon Sauce

Ingredients
1 cup heavy cream
1/4 cup dry white wine
2 tbsp fresh lemon juice
2 tbsp fresh dill, finely chopped
1 shallot, minced
1 clove garlic, minced
1/2 tsp salt
1/4 tsp black pepper

Instructions
 1. In a saucepan over medium heat, sauté shallot and garlic until fragrant (about 1–2 minutes).
 2. Pour in white wine and simmer for 2–3 minutes to reduce slightly.
 3. Add heavy cream and bring to a gentle simmer.
 4. Stir in lemon juice, salt, and pepper.
 5. Let sauce simmer for 3–5 minutes until slightly thickened.
 6. Remove from heat and stir in fresh dill.
 7. Serve warm over salmon dishes.

Perfect for: grilled salmon, salmon Wellington, salmon cakes, gnocchi, wraps & more.

text:
What are the things you should look for on a location scout? 

1. I always like to roam around the space with @cadrageapp to find the best frames. This app allows you to type in your specific camera and lens to see exactly how the frame is gonna look on certain focal lengths. 

2. Established where you’re lighting from. Do windows need to be blacked out? Do you need to light the second story? This will help dictate how long it’ll take to set up a certain room and will help you figure out blocking. 

3. Use Sun Seeker to find where the sun is going to be at certain times of day. This helped us specifically when I wanted a certain shot to take place during blue hour. That meant we needed to be facing a certain direction at a certain time of day. This will help you schedule your day.

4. Find out where it’s safe for crew to stash gear, hold talent, have HMU & wardrobe. If you’re shooting in every single room of a location, it’s going to be an absolute nightmare to move everything for every move. Speaking from experience here! 

5. As much as we’d all like our creativity to flourish on the shoot day, they’re just isn’t enough time to have that luxury most of the time. So do your homework, figure it out in preproduction, go to the location beforehand and map out every detail possible.

🔍 The Story Behind This Painting 👇
🎨 "Massacre of the Innocents" by Léon Cogniet (1824)
🏛️ Location: Musée des Beaux-Arts d’Orléans, Orléans, France

This 19th century painting shows a barefoot woman crouched in terror, her child clutched tightly to her chest, her hand covering his mouth. She stares directly at us, eyes wide,not with fear, but with the silent scream of someone watching death approach. Behind her, chaos unfolds as mothers flee down the stairs, soldiers in pursuit.

Biblical Background
This scene refers to the biblical Massacre of the Innocents,when King Herod, fearing the prophecy of a newborn king, ordered all baby boys in Bethlehem under the age of two to be killed. It's one of the darkest and most emotionally charged episodes in the New Testament, a moment of senseless cruelty targeting the most vulnerable.

🔥The Security Council is the most authoritative organ of the UN.

The structure of the Security Council was designed to reflect the global power hierarchy emerging from the Second World War.

The 5 permanent members (P5) were granted special privileges, including the veto power.

The veto power allows a P5 member to block any decision.

The veto power shaped Cold War dynamics, as the US and the Soviet Union often used it to block each other’s interests.

It also undermined the response to conflicts and humanitarian crises in recent years.

text:
This fascinating optical illusion, known as the “Illusion of S*x,” was created by psychology professor Richard Russell in 2009. It demonstrates how our brains use visual cues—specifically contrast—to determine gender. In the image, the two faces are identical, but increasing the contrast (making the eyes and lips darker while the skin stays light) causes the brain to perceive a female face, while lowering the contrast results in a male perception. 

This happens because, biologically, female faces generally have higher natural contrast between their features and their skin than male faces. By artificially manipulating these levels, Russell proved that contrast is a primary “cue” for gender recognition. This research also helps explain why cosmeticsare such a universal human practice; by darkening the eyes and lips, makeup heightens that contrast, subtly emphasizing feminine traits.

text:
Did you know that, according to a popular theory, the bride in 'The Unequal Marriage' was actually the painter Pukirev's crush? Look closely and you'll see him standing behind the bride, angrily glaring at the groom
This captivating scene shows a young woman on the brink of marrying a wealthy prince. But there is more to this painting than meets the eye!
As we dive into the canvas we uncover the artist's own struggles woven into the masterpiece. According to the story, artist Pukirev himself was fresh out of school and was grappling with his passion for painting while his beloved walked down the aisle with another.
Take a closer look at the characters. Notice the discontent etched into the man on the right? Well, that’s because the man is Pukirev. He’s seen accurately showing his inner turmoil at watching his beloved get married to another man. 
And what about the groom? Well, the elderly man with his stiff demeanor hints at troubles ahead and it contrasts sharply with the bride's innocence.
Amidst the celebration though, you’ll also find two mysterious figures lurking in the background. Who are they? Relatives? Ghosts from the past? 
One popular theory is that these women could be the groom's ex-wives very well casting a shadow over the joyous occasion.

text:
Fish don’t behave randomly. Every pause, turn, follow, or refusal is tied to energy, safety, and opportunity. When you understand that, fishing stops feeling unpredictable and starts making sense. What looks “weird” to us is usually the most efficient choice for the fish.

A fish sitting still is conserving calories. A fish facing current is letting food and oxygen come to it. A fish that follows your lure without biting isn’t rejecting it — it’s evaluating whether the effort is worth the reward. These behaviors are information, not mistakes.

The better angler isn’t the one who fishes harder or faster. It’s the one who reads behavior and adjusts. Once you start asking why a fish is doing something, you stop guessing — and start learning.

#krausfishing #viral #fishing #weird #explained

text:
On the back of my last post,…when in much of this world we’re consistently given a very different message.

For generations we have been culturally fed a message that our body needs outside support, especially our feet.  Mostly from the footwear industry but that message is actively endorsed by most health professionals in regards to both footwear and a reliance on regular needs for treatment from “health” pros.

Education, or should that be reeducation, is the key.

My daughter was researching her persuasive talk to her class about barefoot shoes and told me that all the podiatrists she looked up are advising people to wear heeled, stiff and cushioned shoes.  She was a bit taken aback by this because of the barefoot and minimalist shoe cocoon that she exists in🤣

The podiatrists are taught this and unfortunately most never question it.

As a podiatrist, it is a privilege to have the opportunity to educate people about how their feet and body function naturally.

That they can move and feel better if they understand how their body is designed to behave.

Whether that be coping with pain, building towards a long hike or performing at an elite sport.

Understanding that if given the right environment your body has all it needs within to heal and perform at its best.

It’s a shock to many of us because we’ve only ever been told very differently.  Slowly but surely … these are changing times in the podiatry and shoe world.

Keep an eye on @thefootcollective for events that I help deliver that are powerful education for everyone with feet and those helping people with feet 😆

#naturalpodiatry #podiatry #selfsupport #empowerment #educate #naturalhealing #healnaturally #strongfeet #footstrong

text:
Childhood sexual abuse (CSA) is not something that “stays in the past.” It can shape a person’s life in ways that aren’t always visible. These experiences are part of what is called “Adverse Childhood Experiences”(ACEs). Early traumas that can have lasting effects on mental, emotional, and even physical health.
The long-term impacts of CSA can include:
• Anxiety, depression, and PTSD
• Difficulty with trust, intimacy, and relationships
• Increased risk of substance use or coping behaviors
• Challenges with boundaries and sense of safety
• Chronic health issues 

Healing is possible, but it can often take time, support and understanding. Awareness and compassion matters! 

#wecanpreventcsa #preventcsa #prevent #educate #foryou

A hidden skill that is vital for both reading and writing! 

It’s called crossing the midline, moving a hand, foot, or eye across the invisible line down our body.

This simple movement helps the left & right sides of the brain work together for smooth reading, writing, and coordination. 

Without it, children might:
⚠️ Switch hands mid-task
⚠️ Lose their place while reading
⚠️ Avoid reaching across their body

The BEST part? You can strengthen it through playful, everyday activities.

es protección + ahorro durante toda tu vida.

Pero aquí es donde se pone interesante…

❗ La aportación mensual NO es fija.
Depende de ti… de la cobertura que elijas, de tus metas y de lo que realmente necesitas.
No se trata de un número…
se trata de una estrategia personalizada.

Sí, puede ser más costoso que otras opciones…
pero también te da algo que pocas cosas ofrecen:
✔️ Cobertura de por vida
✔️ Acumulación de dinero

la pregunta es:
👉 ¿Puedes permitirte no tenerlo?

no se trata solo de proteger lo que tienes,

1. 🚨Dealing with a defective product? Here's how to fight back!🔧✅ Your rights, return policies, and warranties explained. Thread for empowerment! #ConsumerRights #ProductWarranty

2. 📜Know Your Rights: Consumer protection laws like the Magnuson-Moss Warranty Act ensure retailers and manufacturers honor their products. #ConsumerLaw

3. 🛠️First Steps: Check the return policy and warranty. If within warranty, you're entitled to a repair or replacement. No warranty? You still have implied warranty rights. #Warranty

4. 📞Manufacturer Contact: If the retailer can't help, reach out to the manufacturer for potential repair, replacement, or refund options. #CustomerService

5. 🔄Return It: Within the return period, return the defective product for a refund or exchange, following the retailer's policy. #ReturnPolicy

6. 📜Warranty Wisdom: Understanding express and implied warranties is key to getting your issue resolved. #WarrantyTypes

7. 🚀Alternative Routes: Traditional methods failed? Explore payment protection, extended warranties, lemon laws, and投诉 effectively. #ConsumerAdvocacy

8. 💳Credit Card Protections: Purchases made with a credit card can offer chargeback options if issues persist. #CreditCardRights

9. 🏛️Legal Action: If all else fails, consider legal action. Understand the process and potential outcomes to seek justice. #LegalAction

10. 🛒Best Practices: Retailers and manufacturers should focus on quality and clear policies. Empower consumers with knowledge! #QualityControl

- CAN earn by:

Educational Thread: Multi-Timeframe Analysis Explained

Most traders fail because they use single timeframe analysis. Here's how to do it right:

1. Weekly (HTF): Defines bias
• Bullish if making higher highs/lows
• Bearish if making lower highs/lows
• Sideways if range-bound

2. Daily: Shows momentum
• Confirms weekly bias
• Identifies trend strength
• Shows institutional accumulation/distribution

3. 4H/1H: Entry timeframe
• Where price action happens
• Shows market structure shifts
• Best for identifying setups

4. 15M: Timing perfection
• Entry refinement
• Stop placement
• Risk management

Key insight: Each TF has its own market microstructure. Don't force HTF levels onto LTF charts.

That's why LeoAgent analyzes each TF independently, then shows how they interact. Clarity > Hopium.

Hi Shawn, Christ dying in place of people is not the same as Christ serving as “penal substitution” for people. Here’s how I just explained it to another person in a thread. 

Let’s say a police officer enters a building to rescue hostages. The kidnappers beat him, torture him, and kill him. His dying words are “forgive them, judge.” At trial, the judge learns of the officer’s dying words and is so moved by the officer’s sacrifice and forgiveness, that he erases the offenders’ sentence if they agree to repent and commit crime no more. This is what Jesus (the officer) and God the Father (the judge) did at Calvary. The police officer is NOT serving the penal sentence in prison for the kidnappers, just as Jesus is NOT serving eternity in hell for us. What Jesus did is sacrifice. It is NOT penal substitution.

text:
#KeralaSurveyFindings2026: ANALYSIS

#BREAKING: TWENTY20 STARES EXISTENTIAL CRISIS IN CENTRAL KERALA.

📌 #EXPLAINER: LET ME BREAK IT DOWN, STEP BY STEP:

[Step 1]
#BACKGROUND & SCENARIO: Twenty20 is a small political group in Kerala, started by a big business company called Kitex Garments. It began as a charity group helping local people with things like cheap food, medicine, and community projects. Over time, it entered politics, promising to stay away from big alliances like the Left (LDF) or Congress-led group (UDF). It focused on being "neutral" and helping ordinary people, especially in rural areas.

[Step 2]

#ARANGETRAM & WHERE THEY STAND TODAY.?

It expanded from its 2015 debut (winning one panchayat) to control four gram panchayats outright, in the 2020 local body elections, marking a strong rural foothold. By 2025, its performance was mixed: it retained control of four gram panchayats but lost two previous strongholds while gaining two new ones. Overall, the party saw a slight increase in total seats won (from around 60+ in 2020 to 78 in 2025), but its expansion into urban areas like Kochi Corporation failed, and vote shares declined in key areas due to opposition alliances. This reflects plateauing influence ahead of the 2026 assembly polls.

[STEP 3]

#INFLUENCE: Twenty20's main power is limited to one area: Ernakulam district in central Kerala. This is where their business is based, and they've built a strong local network through welfare programs like cheap shops and health camps.

[STEP 4]:
#EROSION: Main areas affected: Local bodies (panchayats and blocks) like Kizhakkambalam, Aikkarnadu, Kunnathunad, Mazhuvannur, and Vengola. These are small villages and towns where Twenty20 won elections by promising better daily life (like subsidies on food and clothes).

If leaders quit and take supporters with them, Twenty20 could lose control of these panchayats. For example, in recent local elections, they won seats here, but now resignations could lead to by-elections or shifts in power. Without leaders, they can't run surveys, events, or campaigns effectively in these areas. In short, the loss is mostly local - Ernakulam's rural pockets. The party might shrink back to just being a charity group, not a political force.

📌 Among Which Community Voters Does It Lose Influence?

Twenty20's voters are not from one big group but a mix. They attracted people tired of old parties (LDF and UDF) by focusing on "development" without politics. But their core base is from certain communities, especially minorities who liked their neutral image.

📌 Main communities affected (after joining BJP):

A) Christian voters, especially Jacobite Syrian Christians.
B) Muslim voters.
C) Secular or neutral voters.

Q) Why these communities? Twenty20 promised to dissolve if it ever joined a big alliance. Breaking that promise makes them look dishonest. In #2021_elections, they got about 15% votes in #Ernakulam from these groups. Losing them means dropping to single digits or less.

In simple words:
Christians (especially Jacobites) and Muslims feel tricked (core ideas diluted), while neutral voters feel the party sold out.

text:
১৪ ফেব্রুয়ারি — Season-2, Last Part
সাদা ঘর। নরম আলো। দেয়ালে ঘড়ি — টিক টিক করছে।
রাফি জানালার দিকে তাকিয়ে বসে আছে। সামনে একজন মানুষ নোট লিখছে।
— “আজ কেমন লাগছে?”
কণ্ঠটা শান্ত।
রাফি ধীরে বলল,
— “আজকে প্রথমবার মনে হচ্ছে… আমি তাকে ছেড়ে দিতে পারব।”
লোকটা মাথা নেড়ে কিছু লিখল।
টেবিলের ওপর একটা ফাইল।
ফাইলের কভারে লেখা—
Patient Name: Rafi Hasan
Session: 7
Date: 14 February
লোকটা ফাইল বন্ধ করল।
— “তুমি জানো তো, তিশা ছিল তোমার coping mechanism? ট্রমার পর তোমার মস্তিষ্ক তাকে গল্প বানিয়ে ফিরিয়ে এনেছিল।”
রাফি চুপচাপ শুনছে।
— “Phase_7 ছিল তোমার mind-এর seventh recovery phase. তুমি নিজেকেই মেসেজ পাঠাচ্ছিলে।”
রাফি হালকা হাসল।
— “তাহলে আমি পাগল না?”
লোকটা একটু থেমে বলল,
— “না। তুমি শুধু খুব গভীরভাবে ভালোবেসেছিলে।”
নীরবতা।
ঘড়ির কাঁটা ১০:০০ ছুঁল।
ডাক্তার ফাইলটা তুলে তাকালেন।
তার চোখে অদ্ভুত এক দৃষ্টি।
— “আজকের সেশন শেষ। কিন্তু একটা প্রশ্ন…”
রাফি তাকাল।
ডাক্তার ধীরে বললেন—
— “তুমি কি নিশ্চিত… তিশা সত্যিই শুধু তোমার মাথার ভেতর ছিল?”
রাফির বুক হালকা কেঁপে উঠল।
— “মানে?”
ডাক্তার টেবিলের ড্রয়ার খুললেন।
ভেতর থেকে একটা পুরনো ছবি বের করলেন।
বাসস্ট্যান্ড।
বৃষ্টি।
ছবিতে রাফি দাঁড়িয়ে আছে।
আর তার পাশে—
একটা মেয়ে।
মুখ পরিষ্কার।
হাসছে।
তিশা।
রাফির নিঃশ্বাস আটকে গেল।
— “এটা… কোথায় পেলেন?”
ডাক্তার মৃদু হাসলেন।
— “কারণ সেদিন বাসস্ট্যান্ডে আমি-ও ছিলাম।”
নীরবতা।
ঘড়ির টিকটিক থেমে গেল।
ডাক্তার ধীরে বললেন—
— “আমি তখন তোমাকে বাঁচাতে গিয়েছিলাম।”
রাফির চোখ বড় হয়ে গেল।
— “তাহলে…?”
ডাক্তার সামনে ঝুঁকলেন।
— “কেউ মারা যায়নি সেদিন।”
ঘরটা হঠাৎ ঠান্ডা হয়ে গেল।
— “তাহলে তিশা কোথায়?”
ডাক্তার ধীরে ফিসফিস করে বললেন—
— “এই প্রশ্নটাই তো আমরা সাতটা সেশন ধরে খুঁজছি।”
লাইট হালকা ফ্লিকার করল।
ডাক্তারের টেবিলে থাকা ফাইলটা নিজে থেকে খুলে গেল।
ভেতরে লেখা—
Patient Name: Tisha Rahman
Session: 7
Date: 14 February
রাফি ধীরে মাথা তুলল।
সামনের চেয়ারটা খালি।
ডাক্তার নেই।
ঘরে শুধু সে একা।
টেবিলের ওপরে আয়নায় নিজের প্রতিবিম্ব দেখল—
সে সাদা কোট পরে আছে।
তার হাতে ফাইল।
ফাইলের কভারে লেখা—
Dr. Rafi Hasan
তার ঠোঁট ধীরে নড়ল।
— “আজকের সেশন শেষ, তিশা…”
জানালার বাইরে বৃষ্টি পড়ছে।
ক্যালেন্ডারে আবার একই তারিখ—
১৪ ফেব্রুয়ারি।
শেষ।

১৪ ফেব্রুয়ারি — season-2, episode-7
সময়: অজানা
রাফি স্ক্রিনের দিকে তাকিয়ে আছে।
REMEMBER
RESET
আঙুল থেমে আছে মাঝখানে।
হঠাৎ সে খেয়াল করল… ঘড়িতে আর সময় নেই।
সংখ্যাগুলো ফাঁকা।
ঘর ধীরে ধীরে বদলাতে লাগল।
দেয়াল গলে গিয়ে বাসস্ট্যান্ড হয়ে গেল।
হাওয়া বইছে। বৃষ্টি পড়ছে।
সে আবার সেখানে।
একই দিন।
১৪ ফেব্রুয়ারি।
একটা মেয়ে দাঁড়িয়ে আছে।
এবার মুখ পরিষ্কার।
তিশা।
সে হাসছে না। শুধু তাকিয়ে আছে।
— “শেষ পর্যন্ত মনে পড়েছে?”
রাফির গলা কাঁপছে।
— “আমি… তোকে বাঁচাতে পারিনি…”
তিশা মাথা নেড়ে বলল,
— “তুই আমাকে হারাসনি। তুই শুধু নিজেকে ক্ষমা করতে পারিসনি।”
হঠাৎ চারপাশে মানুষের আওয়াজ ভেসে উঠল।
কিন্তু সবাই ঝাপসা। মুখ নেই।
তিশা ফিসফিস করে বলল,
— “এই জায়গাটা বাস্তব না। এটা তোর মাথার ভেতর।”
রাফি পিছিয়ে গেল।
— “তাহলে Phase_7 কে?”
তিশা ধীরে তার হাতে ফোনটা দিল।
স্ক্রিনে শুধু একটা নাম লেখা—
RAF I — SYSTEM LOG
তার বুক ঠান্ডা হয়ে গেল।
সব মেসেজ… সব টেস্ট…
সব সে নিজেই পাঠিয়েছে।
তার নিজের মস্তিষ্ক, নিজেকে সত্যির দিকে ঠেলে দিচ্ছিল।
হঠাৎ বৃষ্টির শব্দ থেমে গেল।
সবকিছু থেমে আছে।
তিশা বলল,
— “সেদিন আমি তোকে থামাতে গেছিলাম। তুই দৌড়ে রাস্তা পার হতে গিয়ে অ্যাক্সিডেন্ট করলি। আমি চলে গেলাম… কিন্তু তুই বেঁচে গেলি।”
রাফির চোখে পানি।
— “তাহলে এতদিন…”
— “তুই এমন একটা গল্প বানালি যেখানে আমি এখনও আছি। যেখানে সবকিছু happy ending।”
তারপর তিশা ধীরে পিছিয়ে যেতে শুরু করল।
— “এখন সিদ্ধান্ত তোর। আমাকে স্মৃতি হিসেবে রাখবি… নাকি আবার মুছে ফেলবি?”
চারপাশে অন্ধকার নামছে।
ফোনে আবার সেই দুইটা অপশন জ্বলে উঠল—
REMEMBER

রাফি কাঁদতে কাঁদতে বলল,
— “আমি যদি মনে রাখি… তুই কি থাকবে?”
তিশা মৃদু হাসল।
— “আমি কখনো ছিলাম না এখনকার সময়ের জন্য। আমি শুধু তোর অতীত।”
সে আঙুল তুলল…
REMEMBER চাপল।
সব আলো নিভে গেল।
…
ধীরে ধীরে একটা সাদা রুম দেখা গেল।
হাসপাতাল।
একজন ডাক্তার বলছেন,
— “সে অবশেষে বাস্তবতা accept করেছে।”
একজন বন্ধু পাশে বসে।
নাবিল।
সে শান্ত গলায় বলল,
— “ভাই… অনেকদিন পর তুই জেগে উঠলি।”
রাফি চোখ খুলল।
তার পাশে কোনো ফোন নেই।
কোনো Phase_7 নেই।
কিন্তু তার চোখের কোণে জল… আর ঠোঁটে হালকা হাসি।
কারণ এবার সে জানে—
ভালোবাসা সবসময় মানুষকে ধরে রাখে না।
কখনো কখনো… ছেড়ে দিতেও শেখায়।
শেষ… নাকি শুরু?
রাফি জানালার বাইরে তাকাল।
ক্যালেন্ডারে আজকের তারিখ—
১৪ ফেব্রুয়ারি।
কিন্তু সে এবার ভয় পায় না।
কারণ গল্পটা শেষ হয়নি।
এবার সে সত্যি দিয়ে নতুন গল্প লিখবে। 😶
চলবে....

There are worse ways to be remembered than not at all. Not many, but a few.

Some people get a plaque, a line in a book, maybe a story told badly over dinner. A select few get something more enduring—names that slip quietly into everyday use, repeated by people who have no idea who they were and even less interest in finding out.

A sandwich. Nachos. A boycott. A guillotine.

Not legacy so much as… absorption.

text:
Umringt von Jogginganzügen?
Iris Berben, die Grande Dame der deutschen Schauspielkunst, brachte es unlängst auf den Punkt: „Niemand hat mich darauf vorbereitet, 75 zu sein.“ Ja, wenn man die Schwelle von 60 überschritten hat, geht das blitzschnell. 61, 62, 63 – zack ist wieder ein Jahr rum. Die 75 Jahre (auf dem Buckel) rücken näher und näher und näher. Was bedeutet es 75 zu sein? Noch kann ich nicht mitreden. Was ich aber schon weiß, ist, dass der Körper bereits auf dem Weg dorthin eigene Gesetze entwickelt, die man zuvor nicht kannte. Die Gelenke beginnen zu knirschen. Anzunehmen, dass sie dann mit 75 ganze Opernarien singen. Das Gedächtnis, einst ein höchst verlässlicher Partner, wird unzuverlässiger, braucht hin und wieder Nachhilfe. Man fragt sich bang: Wird es mit 75 ein löchriges Sieb sein? Wird man alles vergessen, worauf es ankommt. Und dann das Ding mit der Blase, die einfach den Wecker ersetzt. Brillen werden auch plötzlich unverzichtbar. Schöne Aussichten. Und dann die Konversation. Weniger flüssig als früher. Nette Gesprächspartner nicken verständnisvoll. Weniger nette erinnern einen daran, dass man nun auch noch schlechter hört. Oder man erzählt dieselbe Anekdote zum dritten Mal, nicht nur, weil sie so gut ist, sondern weil man vergessen hat, dass man sie bereits zum Besten gab. Für viele wird auch Bequemlichkeit zum obersten Gebot. Man kommt aus dem Jogginganzug - über den Karl Lagerfeld einst sagte, ein Mensch habe die Kontrolle über sein Leben verloren, wenn er ihn in allen Lebenslagen trage – gar nicht mehr raus . Doch 75 zu sein, ist hoffentlich mehr als das. Beim Blick auf Iris Berben darf man hoffen, und wenn man ihr zuhört, noch mehr. Es scheint auch eine Zeit der Weisheit, der Gelassenheit und der Erkenntnis zu sein. Man hat gelernt, Prioritäten zu setzen und über vieles zu lachen, was einen früher zur Weißglut oder in die Arme von Ernst trieb. Man weiß nun, was wirklich wichtig ist im Leben und schätzt es viel mehr als jemals zuvor. (Foto KI-generiert) #alter #humor😂 #wennmantrotzdemlacht #aufeinneues

text:
Nobody hated Mbappe before he joined Madri really? Lmao 🤣 

When he renewed with Psg in 2022, the backlash from Madrid fans was massive. It got even worse after that interview where he was perceived to have disrespected the club many fans openly said Madrid should never sign him again.

Then there was the constant back-and-forth with Florentino Pérez, which made things even more frustrating. It felt like he was playing with the club for years using their interest as leverage while never making a clear decision.

The repeated transfer saga didn’t help either. Every summer it was the same story, and by 2023, with the contract clause drama at PSG, many fans were already tired of it.

On top of that, the reports about his influence at PSG and the massive contract he signed made some Madrid fans feel like he prioritized money and control over legacy
something that doesn’t align with the club’s values.

It wasn’t just Madrid fans either. A lot of Messi and Neymar Jr. fans grew frustrated with his authoritative attitude at PSG. The constant tension, selfishness, ego and lack of chemistry between them created division within the fanbase, with many feeling he was trying to position himself above player like MESSI and Ney.

Even during the 2022 Champions League run, when Vinícius Júnior scored that iconic goal against Manchester City, commentators were already saying: “Who needs Mbappé? They’ve got Vinicius Jr.”

So no, he was massively hated before he joined us.

text:
📰 Most newsletters are boring AF 😴

Same format. Same predictable structure. Same corporate blah.

Here are 25 newsletter formats that keep readers HOOKED 👇

📖 STORY-DRIVEN:

- The Journey - Weekly progress updates
- Behind the Scenes - Raw, unfiltered truth
- Case Study Deep-dive - One story, many lessons
- The Experiment - Real-time test results
- Day in the Life - Tactical insight as story

🔍 CURATED:

- The Weekly 5 - Best finds of the week
- Signal vs Noise - What matters vs hype
- Trend Report - Industry insights
- The Contrarian - Challenge popular beliefs
- Link Roundup Plus - Links + your hot takes

💬 INTERACTIVE:

- Q&A Style - Answer subscriber questions
- This or That - Compare approaches
- Debate Format - Multiple perspectives
- Reader Spotlight - Feature success stories
- Poll Results - Data + analysis

🎓 EDUCATIONAL:

- The Lesson - One concept, deeply explained
- Mistake Monday - Failures + lessons
- Tool Tutorial - Step-by-step guides
- Framework Friday - Mental models
- Beginner's Guide - Complex made simple

🎭 UNIQUE:

- The Confession - Vulnerable truth-telling
- Devil's Advocate - Argue unpopular sides
- Future Letter - Write from 10 years ahead
- The Apology - When things go wrong
- Random Thoughts - Stream of consciousness

The truth: Format matters more than content. 🎯

A mediocre insight in an engaging format beats brilliant content in a boring structure.

The best newsletters feel like letters from a smart friend, not corporate broadcasts. 💌

Which format matches YOUR personality? Drop it below! 👇

SAVE THIS for newsletter inspiration! 📌

Mein Newsletter ist da. Juhu
Hoffentlich jeden 10. des Monats landet bei dir, wenn du das möchtest eine Newsletter Mail. 
Ich bin im Newsletter-Game noch neu und werde mit der Zeit sicherlich noch das ein oder andere ändern.  Ich werde auf mein Fort- und Weiterbildungsangebot aufmerksam machen und die neuen Termine bekanntgeben.
Immer wieder wird es kleine Gedanken und Ideen geben zu den Themen Sterben, Tod und Trauer.
ch werden verschiedene Personen, Bücher, Unternehmen vorstellen, die ich in der Thematik interessant finde. 
Der erste ist noch ein bisschen knapper, das wird sich aber ändern. 

Wenn du dabei sein möchtest: Link in der Bio – kostenlos, jederzeit abbestellbar, hoffentlich immer am 10. des Monats.

Ich freue mich auf dich.

🗞️ Have you heard about Brighton Institute’s FREE Monthly Newsletter?

What is it?
This is a newsletter that goes out (typically) on the first of each month to subscribers of the Brighton Institute.

What information is included?
In the newsletter, readers will find information about upcoming BI events as well as any other useful information. For example, January's Newsletter had an entire section about the history of the Brighton Institute in honor of our 10 Year Anniversary AND information about an upcoming ski waxing event.

How do I sign up for the newsletter?
To sign up for the newsletter, go to https://www.thebrightoninstitute.org/subscribe.

text:
🎀 Newsletter Alert 🎀 

Did you know I have a monthly(ish) newsletter? It's more like bi-monthly, but who's checking? Point is, I promise not to be annoying. 

Lady James invites you to join the Drawing Room Whispers. Each instalment has a recent book recommendation, writing updates, and publishing news. If you want to be in the loop, the drawing room is the place to be. I've been promising a free short story for a while, but the first one went off the rails and turned into a novella. My current project is a revision of my RWAus Sweet Treats entry that didn't make it into the anthology last year. Turns out I'm better at writing spicy! I enjoyed working the Honey theme into a regency story and now I'm loving turning it into a steamy piece. 

Those signed up to the Drawing Room Whispers will be the first to read it and I'm working really hard to get it finished soon! 

Next newsletter arrives in inboxes on Monday! 

🔗 in bio

📤 Have you checked out our March 2026 newsletter yet? 🎉

This month, we're recapping the Lunch & Learn Session Winter 2026, co-hosted by IWA YWP Canada’s Atlantic and Pacific regions at Memorial University of Newfoundland. With over 60 attendees, the event explored how engineering expertise can shape public policy on water issues. 🌊 We’re also highlighting an exciting upcoming workshop at the CAWQ Symposium on April 9 in Toronto: “Snakes and Ladders: The ‘Game’ of Getting Your Research Idea from Concept to Delivery to Operationalization.” Don’t miss it!

We are also featuring a new blog post by Rhea Bhansali, Founder & CEO of Caelra Capital, on the emerging discipline of Water Finance, exploring how hydrological conditions translate into financial signals and shape global investment decisions. 💡 Plus, check out the Research Spotlight section, highlighting innovative photocatalytic water treatment approaches, from solar-driven selenium removal to immobilized nanomaterials for micropollutant degradation!

Don't miss out on the latest news! Read the full newsletter here: https://tinyurl.com/n4ubrnw7 

Calling all YWPs across Canada! This is your opportunity to connect with us. Want to stay in the loop about our upcoming events? Looking for information on opportunities, awards, and jobs? Subscribing to our newsletter is the key to staying informed! Subscribe now: https://tinyurl.com/mr44db8y 

Would you like to contribute to our monthly newsletter or share exciting news and opportunities with our community? Send us an email at iwa.canada.ywp@gmail.com.

Editors: Hadi Mokarizadeh, PhD, E.I.T | Dafne de Brito Cruz | Isaac Sánchez-Montes | Edgar Martín Hernández | Nimitha Choran

IKEA e l’IA: la lezione da 1 miliardo. 
Mentre molte aziende usano l’intelligenza artificiale per tagliare i costi, IKEA l’ha usata per aumentare il valore umano.
Hanno riqualificato 8.500 dipendenti, trasformando gli operatori del call center in consulenti d’arredo. 

Il risultato? Un miliardo di dollari di fatturato aggiuntivo.
Il punto non è resistere al cambiamento, ma capire dove si sposta il valore e arrivarci prima degli altri. 

L’IA gestisce la routine, tu gestisci la strategia e la creatività.

Ogni domenica approfondisco casi come questo e le novità del digitale nella mia newsletter per professionisti e imprenditori.

Save the Date | INDIA'S LARGEST GLOBAL TEXTILE EVENT IS BACK! Bharat Tex 2026 - 14 -17 July 2026

Bharat Tex 2026 invites the global textile community to come together at India's most comprehensive textile platform, bringing the entire value chain under one roof. text:
St. Francis Institute of Management Studies organized an industrial visit on 19 January 2026 for the first-year students to the Moglix warehouse, Shamirpet, Hyderabad, offering a valuable opportunity to witness industry operations firsthand.

Founded in 2015 by Rahul Garg, Moglix is a leading industrial procurement and supply chain solutions company, with its global headquarters in Singapore, strategically positioned at a major export–import and technology hub. The visit provided students with practical insights into B2B and B2C procurement models, surface logistics, and technology-enabled fulfillment systems supporting diverse industries, including pharmaceuticals.

The interaction enabled students to bridge classroom learning with real-world applications, gaining a deeper understanding of modern supply chain management and large-scale logistics operations. Such industry engagements play a vital role in enhancing experiential learning and preparing students for the evolving demands of the global business environment

On World Engineers' Day, aerospace perfectly illustrates the link between engineering and economic performance. Airbus ended 2025 with record results, delivering 793 aircraft and confirming the recovery of civil aviation.

What factors explain this momentum?
➡️ Robust recovery in global demand, supporting production rates
➡️ Integration of advanced technologies and innovations on assembly lines
➡️ Optimization of post-pandemic supply chains
➡️ Ongoing commitment to the performance and reliability of aerospace systems

Safety, technical excellence, and industrial complexity make engineering a central pillar for balancing growth, competitiveness, and technological innovation.

text:
Throwback Thursday to earlier this week, when I got to welcome my fellow LMG Leading Lady and friend Connie to the office.

It only took about 1.5 years of planning, but we finally made it happen 😂

We met 4 years ago, at my very first #LMG Leading Ladies event in the “hot tub” as part of the ice-breaker welcome event (thnx Suzi 😅🤭)
Clearly, that solidified the friendship; that we’ve booked every annual Leading Ladies trip together ever since.

There are many things I love about our industry but the most important one is the people you meet along the way. They are not “industry colleagues” or competition. They are collaborators, sounding boards, and genuine friendships who understand the highs and lows of running a business because they are living it too!

Thank you for coming down, Connie. We loved having you at the Trusted Financial Choice office, and thanks for spoiling the team and I with delicious treats 🍰 🥐

text:
BROOKLYN, NEW YORK — Two NYPD officers have been placed on modified duty as an internal investigation continues into a video showing an arrest at a Brooklyn liquor store, according to officials.

Police said the incident occurred Tuesday afternoon inside a store near Hoyt and Baltic streets in Cobble Hill. The video shows officers attempting to detain a man, with portions of the encounter drawing public attention online.

NYPD Commissioner Jessica Tisch said the video is part of an ongoing internal review. Officials have not released full details about the circumstances leading up to the arrest.

Authorities said undercover narcotics detectives had been investigating a suspected drug transaction involving the individual. No drugs were recovered during the incident, and the man was issued a desk appearance ticket for charges including resisting arrest and obstruction of governmental administration, according to police.

The Brooklyn District Attorney’s Office later indicated the charges would be dismissed.

Officials said the officer seen continuing to strike the man after he was restrained has been relieved of his gun and shield pending the outcome of the investigation.

City officials described the video as “extremely disturbing” and said the department is conducting a full review.

The investigation remains ongoing.

#Brooklyn #NYC #Breaking

📉🏡 Market Update: Buyers Pausing Ahead of the Budget

The property market has definitely slowed over the past few weeks — and it’s largely down to one thing: uncertainty around the upcoming Autumn Budget. Many buyers and sellers are choosing to wait for clarity before making their move. 🤔💭

Industry leaders say this Budget is having the same impact on confidence as a general election. The positive side? Once the details drop, activity usually rebounds quickly — meaning we could see an unusually busy December and early January. 📈✨

Here’s what we’re seeing across the UK right now:
✨ Buyer caution is high— people want clarity before committing.
🏷️ Accurate pricing is crucial — realistically priced homes are still attracting interest.
👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 Needs-based moves are driving the market — first-time buyers, relocators, and families still making essential moves.
🏡 Larger homes are taking longer— often needing price adjustments.
🤝 Buyers have more negotiating power— and they’re using it.

And importantly…
💡 Having a clear plan for the post-Budget market is key.

Whether the updates bring incentives or challenges, being prepared to act will help you make the most of the renewed activity that often follows. 🔑📅

If you’re thinking of selling or buying, staying informed — and planning ahead — will put you in the strongest position. 💬🏡

Market Update: PVC Foam Board Prices Rise by Around 20%

The global market is currently witnessing a significant increase in PVC Foam Board prices, with many suppliers reporting an average 20% price rise in recent weeks.

Key reasons behind the increase:

• PVC resin prices have increased globally, driven by higher crude oil and petrochemical costs. 
• International shipping costs have surged, with container freight on Asia–Europe routes rising due to longer shipping routes and higher insurance costs. 
• Supply chain disruptions and port delays are also tightening availability in several markets.

Logistics impact: 
Shipping costs from Asia to Europe have increased 20–40% compared to earlier months, adding additional pressure to building material prices.

Market outlook: 
Industry analysts expect the PVC market to remain volatile over the next 2–3 months. If shipping disruptions and raw material shortages continue, prices could increase a further 5–10% before stabilizing.

PVC Foam Boards are widely used in construction, interior design, signage, and furniture, so these price changes may influence project costs across multiple industries.

#PVC #ConstructionMaterials #MarketUpdate #SupplyChain #GlobalTrade

text:
🚨 MARKET UPDATE: Gold Prices Slide Amid Rate Fears & Strong Dollar

Gold prices are taking a hit, falling to multi-month lows as investors react to shifting global economic signals.

Recent reports highlight that the sell-off is being driven by a mix of rising inflation concerns, higher bond yields, and a stronger U.S. dollar—all of which are reducing gold’s appeal as a safe-haven asset.

📉 Key drivers behind the decline:

• Hawkish Federal Reserve outlook – expectations of higher interest rates are making non-yielding assets like gold less attractive
• Stronger U.S. dollar – pushing investors toward dollar-based assets instead
• Rising bond yields – offering better returns compared to gold
• Profit-taking after a strong rally – gold surged earlier this year, prompting investors to lock in gains
• Weakening investor sentiment – outflows from major gold ETFs signal declining demand

Despite the downturn, analysts note that gold is still significantly higher year-on-year, and some see the pullback as a potential buying opportunity in the long term.

📊  Bottom line:
Gold’s recent drop shows that even traditional “safe haven” assets can struggle when interest rates rise and capital shifts toward higher-yield investments.

🏡 Market update! 🏡

The mortgage market has shown resilience despite wider political and economic uncertainty. In August, there were 64,680 approvals, just 0.7% down on July, a modest dip for what is usually a quieter month in the housing calendar. 🙌

Monthly changes have been minimal, shifting by fewer than 1,000 over the past three months, with current levels broadly in line with six months ago.
Mortgage rates have remained steady since the Bank of England chose to hold interest rates at its September meeting, underlining overall stability in the market. Activity levels remain consistent, with most borrowers now largely adapting to the current rate environment. 💰

🏘 Despite this, demand may ease in the run-up to the autumn budget, especially at the top end of the market. Many high-end buyers are choosing to hold back until there is more certainty around possible tax changes, which could temporarily slow activity at the top of the market. 

Source: Dataloft by PriceHubble, Bank of England

text:
🏡Market Update for November 2025🏡

🏠 Housing market sees little change as year-end nears.

“As the year draws to a close, the data continues telling a story of a market with many buyers patiently waiting and sellers adjusting to market conditions not seen in years. Inventory remains healthy, providing buyers ample choice, which, by contrast, is pushing sellers to accept that pricing must reflect this new reality. Buyers and sellers are striking deals when their expectations are aligned and reflective of the current market – not the market of years ago,”, Andrew Lis said.

💰 November 2025 residential sales totaled:

⬇️ 1,846 in November 2025, a 15.4% decrease from the 2,181 sales recorded in November 2024
⬇️ 20.6 per cent below the 10-year seasonal average (2,324)

“As sales volumes remain subdued and inventory remains plentiful, properties are taking longer to sell, and pricing has continued to soften slightly across most market segments,” Lis said. “With borrowing costs likely to remain steady into the new year, any uptick in demand will need to arise from a significant change in buyer sentiment. As December is typically among the quietest months of the year in terms of market activity, the prevailing trends suggest we should expect a quiet close to a year marked by considerable uncertainty.”

🏡Market Update for October 2025🏡

Slow sales and high inventory give buyers the edge in October.

“October is typically the last month of the year where sales activity sees a seasonal uptick, but sales still fell short of last year’s figures and the ten-year seasonal average. Even the fourth cut this year to the Bank of Canada’s policy rate this October wasn’t enough to entice more buyers back into the market,” Andrew Lis said.

💰 October 2025 residential sales totalled:
⬇️ 2,255, a 14.3% decrease from last year;
⬇️ 14.5% below than the seasonal average (2,638).

“After peaking in June, inventory levels have edged lower, and prices have eased across all market segments as slower-than-usual sales activity meets the highest inventory levels seen in many years,” Lis said. “With no further reductions to the Bank of Canada’s policy rate expected in 2025, market conditions appear as favorable for buyers as they’ve been all year.”

text:
🚨 MARKET UPDATE: Oil Ticks Higher Amid Iran–U.S. Talks

Global oil prices are showing slight gains as markets closely watch developments between the United States and Iran.

Recent reports reveal that Iran is reviewing a U.S. proposal to end the ongoing conflict, creating mixed signals in the energy market. While the possibility of peace talks initially pushed prices lower, renewed uncertainty has led to a modest rebound in oil prices.

📊 Key Highlights:

Oil prices had earlier dropped around 2%–7% amid optimism over a potential ceasefire.
Prices are now ticking slightly higher as no final agreement has been reached.
The conflict continues to threaten supply, especially through the Strait of Hormuz, a key global oil route.

⚠️ Why It Matters:

About 20% of the world’s oil supply passes through this region, making any disruption highly impactful.
Markets remain volatile, balancing hope for peace and fear of escalation.

💡 Bottom Line:
Oil prices are currently driven by geopolitical uncertainty—any confirmation of a ceasefire could push prices down, while further conflict may trigger another spike.

text:
📰 BREAKING | INDUSTRY WATCH

Automation in Textile Manufacturing Goes Viral — What the Videos Show, and What They Leave Out

Viral clips of near-empty textile factories are spreading fast, fuelling claims that automation has already wiped out human labour. The visuals are real. The conclusions are not. Here’s a fact-first breakdown of what’s actually happening inside modern textile manufacturing.

🔴 WHAT’S CONFIRMED
•Automation is accelerating globally, led by China and other major manufacturing hubs.
•AI, robotics, sensors, and smart systems are now standard in high-volume textile plants.
•Viral videos showing few or no workers on the shop floor are authentic.
•These facilities operate under Industry 4.0 models—continuous, data-driven production.

🟠 “UNMANNED FACTORIES” — REAL, BUT LIMITED
•Some plants run with minimal visible labour, mainly in:
•Weaving and spinning
•Fabric cutting
•Dyeing and finishing
•Humans haven’t disappeared—they’ve moved off the floor:
•Maintenance engineers
•Control-room operators
•Automation and software technicians
•Quality and logistics staff
•Routine operator roles are shrinking; the system is not human-free.

🟡 WHAT MACHINES REPLACE — AND WHAT THEY DON’T

Highly automatable
•Weaving, spinning
•Cutting
•Dyeing/finishing
•AI-based visual inspection

Still human-dependent
•Complex stitching
•Design changes
•Custom garments
•Small-batch and artisanal work

🟢 JOB IMPACT: DISPLACEMENT VS TRANSFORMATION
•Low-skill, repetitive roles are declining where automation scales fast.
•Output is rising; employment is stagnant or falling in some regions.
•New jobs are growing in:
•Machine maintenance
•Systems monitoring
•Data and industrial IT
•Robotics and automation support
•Risk is highest where reskilling and transition support are weak.

⚠️ WHY THESE VIDEOS GO VIRAL
•Empty floors create high-impact visuals.
•Short clips omit context and are often:
•Promotional showcases
•Selectively framed
•Misread as industry-wide norms
•One factory ≠ the entire global textile sector.

❌ MYTHS VS ✔️ REALITY
•❌ “Automation means no human jobs”
•❌ “AI has already erased textile employment”
•❌ “One viral plant represents the whole industry”
•✔️ Automation reduces some roles, shifts others
•✔️ Most factories run human–machine collaboration

📌 WHAT REALLY MATTERS NEXT
•Automation is not optional for competitiveness.
•The decisive factors now are:
•Worker reskilling
•Education aligned to industrial change
•Policy planning for labour transitions
•Countries that prepare workers adapt.
•Those that don’t face inequality—not instant joblessness.

🔚 BOTTOM LINE
•The machines are real.
•The transformation is real.
•The claim that AI has already ended human work is not.

Automation isn’t the end of jobs.
It’s the end of old job definitions.
What happens next depends on people—not machines.

#Automation
#Manufacturing
#FutureOfWork
